# Trip AI Planner — Copilot Instructions

## Project Overview

AI-powered travel planner for Asia-Pacific destinations. Users search origin → destination, select routes generated by Gemini AI, view detailed multi-day itineraries on an interactive map, get hotel/flight recommendations, and estimate trip costs. Supports Vietnamese (default) and English with VND/USD currencies.

## Core Principles

- **YAGNI** — Only build what's needed now
- **KISS** — Prefer simple, readable solutions over clever abstractions
- **DRY** — Extract shared logic into hooks/utils/services, not copy-paste
- **SOLID** — Single responsibility per file; depend on abstractions (stores, services)
- **Mobile-first** — Design for mobile, enhance for desktop
- **Server-safe keys** — Never expose API keys to the browser; all AI calls go through `/api/ai`

## Tech Stack

| Layer      | Technology                                                          |
| ---------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Framework  | Next.js 15 (App Router, standalone output)                          |
| Language   | TypeScript (ES2022, bundler resolution, `@/*` path alias)           |
| UI         | React 19, Tailwind CSS 3, Framer Motion                             |
| Components | Radix UI (Dialog, Popover, Slot), Lucide React icons, Vaul (drawer) |
| State      | Zustand 5 (with `persist` middleware for auth)                      |
| AI         | Google Gemini (`@google/genai`, model: `gemini-3-flash-preview`)    |
| Auth       | Firebase Auth (email/password + Google OAuth)                       |
| Maps       | Leaflet 1.9 (dynamic import, SSR-disabled)                          |
| i18n       | Custom `translations` object in `src/utils/i18n.ts`                 |
| Weather    | Visual Crossing API                                                 |
| Styling    | `cn()` helper using `clsx` + `tailwind-merge`                       |
| Testing    | Playwright                                                          |
| Formatting | Prettier                                                            |
| Deployment | Vercel (also Docker + Nginx available)                              |

## Development Guidelines

### File Organization & Naming

- **Components:** PascalCase (`SearchPanel.tsx`, `HotelCard.tsx`)
- **Hooks:** camelCase with `use` prefix (`useMediaQuery.ts`)
- **Stores:** camelCase with `Store` suffix (`tripStore.ts`, `appStore.ts`)
- **Services:** camelCase with `Service` suffix (`geminiService.ts`, `weatherService.ts`)
- **Utils:** camelCase (`currency.ts`, `i18n.ts`)
- **Types:** Centralized in `src/types.ts` and `src/types/` directory
- **Barrel exports:** Use `index.ts` in feature folders

### Project Structure

```
app/                    # Next.js App Router
  layout.tsx            # Root layout (Inter font, MobileGestureProvider)
  page.tsx              # Entry — renders <App />
  globals.css           # Tailwind directives, Leaflet CSS, custom scrollbar
  api/
    ai/route.ts         # AI proxy (Gemini) — protects API key
    weather/route.ts    # Weather proxy (Visual Crossing)
src/
  App.tsx               # Main client component (feature routing, auth guard)
  types.ts              # Shared type definitions
  components/
    features/
      auth/             # AuthPage
      trip-planner/     # SearchPanel, RouteSelector, Timeline, Hotels, Flights, Cost
      weather/          # WeatherDashboard, CurrentWeather, ForecastList
    layout/             # AppShell, Header, Sidebar, BottomNav
    providers/          # MobileGestureProvider
    ui/                 # DatePicker (reusable primitives)
    Icons.tsx           # CategoryIcon, getCategoryColor
    Map.tsx             # Leaflet map wrapper
  hooks/                # useMediaQuery, useIsMobile, useIsDesktop
  lib/
    firebase.ts         # Client-side Firebase init
    firebase-admin.ts   # Server-side Firebase Admin
    utils.ts            # cn() helper
  services/
    aiClient.ts         # Browser → /api/ai fetch wrapper
    geminiService.ts    # Server-side Gemini calls (structured output)
    promptService.ts    # All AI prompt templates
    placesService.ts    # Place autocomplete (local JSON)
    geolocationService.ts
    weatherService.ts
  stores/               # Zustand stores
    appStore.ts         # activeFeature, language, currency, sidebar, online
    tripStore.ts        # search, routes, itinerary, hotels, flights, cost
    authStore.ts        # user, guestPromptCount, isAuthForced (persisted)
    weatherStore.ts
  utils/
    currency.ts         # VND/USD formatting (formatCurrency, formatCurrencyCompact)
    i18n.ts             # translations object (vi, en)
```

### Code Quality Standards

**TypeScript:**

- Use interfaces for object shapes, enums for fixed sets (`TravelMode`, `StopType`)
- Define types in `src/types.ts`; feature-specific types in `src/types/`
- Use the `@/*` path alias for imports from `src/`

**React patterns:**

- Mark client components with `'use client'` when using hooks or browser APIs
- Use `dynamic()` with `{ ssr: false }` for browser-only libs (Leaflet)
- Prefer functional components with `React.FC<Props>`
- Destructure props in the function signature

**Hooks:**

- Custom hooks go in `src/hooks/`
- SSR-safe: always guard `window`/`navigator` with `typeof window !== 'undefined'`

### UI Primitives — Always Use These

| Component       | Path                         | Use Instead Of                 |
| --------------- | ---------------------------- | ------------------------------ |
| `DatePicker`    | `@/components/ui/DatePicker` | Raw `<input type="date">`      |
| `CategoryIcon`  | `@/components/Icons`         | Inline icon switch statements  |
| `cn()`          | `@/lib/utils`                | Manual className concatenation |
| Radix `Dialog`  | `@radix-ui/react-dialog`     | Custom modal implementations   |
| Radix `Popover` | `@radix-ui/react-popover`    | Custom dropdown/tooltip        |
| `Vaul` Drawer   | `vaul`                       | Custom bottom sheet            |
| Lucide icons    | `lucide-react`               | Raw SVGs or other icon libs    |

### State Management

- Use **Zustand** for all global state — one store per domain
- Access stores via hooks: `useAppStore()`, `useTripStore()`, `useAuthStore()`
- Use `persist` middleware only when data must survive page reload (e.g., `authStore`)
- Keep stores flat; derive computed values in components
- Never import stores in server components

### Styling Conventions

- **Tailwind CSS** for all styling — no CSS modules, no styled-components
- Use `cn()` for conditional classes: `cn('base-class', condition && 'active-class')`
- Mobile breakpoint: `md:` (768px) — design mobile-first
- Use Tailwind's `animate-*` utilities and custom keyframes in `tailwind.config.js`
- Font: Inter (loaded via `next/font/google`)
- Color palette: Slate for neutrals, Blue/Indigo for primary, contextual colors for categories

### Backend / API Integration

- **Client → Server pattern:** Browser calls `aiClient.ts` → `POST /api/ai` → `geminiService.ts`
- Never import `geminiService.ts` or use `process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY` on the client
- All AI responses use Gemini's structured output (`responseSchema`) for type safety
- Prompts are centralized in `promptService.ts` — never inline prompt strings
- Auth: Attach Firebase ID token as `Bearer` in `Authorization` header
- Guest users get 3 free prompts before auth wall

### i18n

- Translation keys live in `src/utils/i18n.ts` as a `translations` object with `vi` and `en` keys
- Access via `const t = translations[language]` where `language` comes from `useAppStore()`
- Default language: Vietnamese (`vi`)
- All user-visible strings must go through translations — never hardcode UI text
- When adding a new string: add keys to both `vi` and `en` in `i18n.ts`

## Component Templates

### New Feature Component

```tsx
'use client';

import React from 'react';
import { cn } from '@/lib/utils';
import { translations } from '@/utils/i18n';
import { useAppStore } from '@/stores/appStore';

interface MyFeatureProps {
  className?: string;
}

const MyFeature: React.FC<MyFeatureProps> = ({ className }) => {
  const { language } = useAppStore();
  const t = translations[language];

  return <div className={cn('p-4', className)}>{/* content */}</div>;
};

export default MyFeature;
```

### Custom Hook

```tsx
import { useState, useEffect } from 'react';

export function useMyHook(param: string) {
  const [value, setValue] = useState<string | null>(null);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (typeof window === 'undefined') return;
    // browser-only logic
  }, [param]);

  return value;
}
```

### Service Layer

```ts
// Server-side service (called from API routes only)
export async function fetchSomething(input: string): Promise<Result> {
  // Use process.env secrets here
  const response = await fetch(externalUrl);
  return response.json();
}
```

## Testing & Quality

- **Playwright** for E2E tests
- Run: `npx playwright test`
- Format: `npm run format` (Prettier)
- Format check: `npm run format:check`
- Build validation: `npm run build`

## Security Guidelines

- API keys (`GEMINI_API_KEY`, `VISUAL_CROSSING_API_KEY`, Firebase Admin) are server-side only
- Client-side Firebase config uses `NEXT_PUBLIC_` prefix — these are safe to expose
- Verify Firebase ID tokens in API routes via `firebase-admin`
- Sanitize all user input before passing to AI prompts
- Guest rate limiting: 3 prompts max before requiring authentication

## Performance Best Practices

- Dynamic import Leaflet with `ssr: false` — it requires browser APIs
- Use `next/font` for font optimization (Inter)
- Next.js standalone output for minimal Docker images
- Lazy-load heavy features (map, weather dashboard)
- Keep Zustand stores minimal — avoid storing derived data
- Use `useCallback`/`useMemo` only when profiling shows a need

## Common Patterns

- **Feature routing:** `useAppStore().activeFeature` switches between trip-planner, weather, budget, etc. in `App.tsx`
- **Mobile/desktop adaptation:** `useIsMobile()` hook → conditionally render Header, BottomNav, Sidebar
- **Place autocomplete:** Local JSON files in `public/data/` searched via `placesService.ts`
- **Cost estimation:** Users select itinerary items, hotels, flights → `CostSummary` computes totals
- **Currency formatting:** Always store amounts in VND, convert at display time via `formatCurrency()`
- **AI structured output:** Gemini `responseSchema` ensures typed JSON responses

## What NOT to Do

- ❌ Import `geminiService.ts` or `firebase-admin.ts` in client components
- ❌ Use `process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY` or `VISUAL_CROSSING_API_KEY` on the client
- ❌ Hardcode UI strings — always use `translations[language]`
- ❌ Use raw `<input>`, `<button>`, `<select>` when a UI primitive exists
- ❌ Create new CSS files — use Tailwind classes
- ❌ Add state to Zustand stores that can be derived from existing state
- ❌ Use `useEffect` for data fetching in new code — prefer service layer pattern
- ❌ Skip `'use client'` directive when using hooks or browser APIs
- ❌ Inline AI prompt text — put all prompts in `promptService.ts`
- ❌ Store amounts in USD — always VND internally, convert at display

## Environment Variables

**Server-only (never prefix with `NEXT_PUBLIC_`):**

- `GEMINI_API_KEY`
- `VISUAL_CROSSING_API_KEY`
- `FIREBASE_ADMIN_*` (service account credentials)

**Client-safe (`NEXT_PUBLIC_` prefix):**

- `NEXT_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_API_KEY`
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN`
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID`
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET`
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID`
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_APP_ID`

## Quick Reference

| Action          | Command                   |
| --------------- | ------------------------- |
| Dev server      | `npm run dev` (port 4200) |
| Build           | `npm run build`           |
| Start prod      | `npm start` (port 4200)   |
| Format          | `npm run format`          |
| Format check    | `npm run format:check`    |
| Generate places | `npm run generate:places` |
| E2E tests       | `npx playwright test`     |

### Adding a New Feature Checklist

1. Create component in `src/components/features/<feature-name>/`
2. Add translation keys to `src/utils/i18n.ts` (both `vi` and `en`)
3. Add feature type to `FeatureType` union in `appStore.ts` if it's a top-level feature
4. Add navigation entry in `Sidebar.tsx` and `BottomNav.tsx`
5. Add feature rendering case in `App.tsx`
6. If it needs AI: add prompt in `promptService.ts`, schema + service function in `geminiService.ts`, client wrapper in `aiClient.ts`, API route handler in `app/api/ai/route.ts`
7. Export from barrel `index.ts`
